methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian is a public-stage transportation company currently valued at $20.7B, having raised $13.5B to date. The transportation sector peer universe comprises five private comparables—The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace—with no public peers identified in the narrow comp set. The absence of same-stage and adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum density thresholds (requiring ≥3 datapoints) limits traditional peer-median applicability.

The peer-median method is not applicable in this case. The transportation sector contains only four same-sector peers, yet none satisfy the filtering criteria for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability. Requiring a minimum of three datapoints to establish a meaningful median, the narrow peer set falls short. Employing an all-sector median would introduce substantial cross-sector dispersion and misrepresent Rivian's specific capital intensity and commercialization timeline relative to its direct competition.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable set and absent triangulation across multiple methodologies, this analysis cannot responsibly synthesize a derived valuation range or confidence bounds. The key constraint is the scarcity of mature, capital-efficient peers at comparable developmental and market stages. Further analysis would require either expansion to adjacent mobility or infrastructure verticals with documented risk-return profiles, or reliance on alternative approaches such as discounted cash flow analysis anchored to production roadmap and unit economics assumptions—neither of which are present in the current dataset.
